questions.title: Variable Declaration and Output
questions.description: Write a JavaScript program that declares a variable, assigns it a value from input, and prints it using console.log.
questions.explanation: Use readline or similar to read input, store in variable using let or const, and output with console.log.
hints: Store input in variable if needed

questions.title: String Length
questions.description: Write a JavaScript program that reads a string and prints its length using the .length property.
questions.outputFormat: Print the length of the string.
questions.explanation: Read string, access .length property to get character count, and print the result.

questions.title: Array Sum
questions.description: Write a JavaScript program that reads N integers on a single line separated by spaces, stores them in an array, and calculates the sum of all elements using reduce method or loop.
questions.inputFormat: N space-separated integers on a single line.
questions.outputFormat: Print the sum of all integers.
questions.explanation: Split input by spaces, convert to numbers, use reduce() or loop to sum all elements.
hints: Use map(Number) to convert to numbers

questions.title: Object Property Access
questions.description: Write a JavaScript program that creates an object with properties name, age, and city. Read values for these properties from input (each on separate line), then print the object's name property.
questions.explanation: Create object using object literal, assign properties from input, access property using dot notation.

questions.title: Array Filter and Map
questions.description: Write a JavaScript program that reads N integers, filters to keep only even numbers, maps them to their squares, and prints the results separated by spaces. If no even numbers, print 0.
questions.inputFormat: N space-separated integers on a single line.
questions.outputFormat: Print squared even numbers separated by spaces, or 0 if none.
questions.explanation: Use filter() to get even numbers (num % 2 === 0), use map() to square them, join with spaces.
hints: Use map() to square: num => num * num
